old_value:
{"id":"FREGMA-7k8","title":"Add dataType and isPreferred support to LLM extraction pipeline","description":"The LLM extraction pipeline (packages/llm) does not currently extract dataType for value types or mark isPreferred on uniqueness constraints. Models extracted from transcripts are missing this information, which affects relational mapping quality (PK types default to TEXT, preferred identifiers fall back to heuristic).

Required changes:
- Add data_type field to ExtractedObjectType in ExtractionTypes.ts
- Add is_preferred field to InferredConstraint for internal_uniqueness constraints
- Update ExtractionPrompt to instruct the LLM to infer data types for value types and mark preferred identifiers
- Update buildResponseSchema() to include data_type and is_preferred in the JSON schema sent to the LLM
- Update DraftModelParser to propagate dataType when creating object types
- Update constraint parsing to propagate isPreferred
- Add tests for all new extraction paths","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"gschenz@ritchiebros.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T04:11:09Z","created_by":"Gabe Schenz","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:11:09Z"}

new_value: Implemented: data_type on ExtractedObjectType, is_preferred on InferredConstraint, prompt/schema/parser updates, 12 new tests. 864 tests passing.

old_value:
{"id":"FREGMA-c16","title":"Add validation warnings for dataType and isPreferred consistency","description":"Add two completeness/consistency validation rules:

1. Warn when a value type has no dataType set. Value types should ideally declare their conceptual data type so the relational mapper can generate accurate column types instead of defaulting to TEXT.

2. Warn when an entity type has zero isPreferred uniqueness constraints (no preferred identifier declared) or more than one isPreferred constraint (conflicting preferred identifiers). This helps catch modeling issues early.

These are non-blocking warnings (not errors) in the validation engine, similar to existing completeness warnings in src/validation/rules/completenessWarnings.ts.

Files to modify:
- src/validation/rules/completenessWarnings.ts (or new file)
- tests/validation/completenessWarnings.test.ts","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"gschenz@ritchiebros.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T04:11:16Z","created_by":"Gabe Schenz","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:11:16Z"}

new_value: Implemented two completeness warning rules for dataType and isPreferred consistency

{"description":"Reverse-engineer an ORM 2 model from an existing dbt project. Infer entity types from model names, fact types from relationships (refs), value types from column definitions, and constraints from tests (unique, not_null, accepted_values). Identify gaps where dbt structure is ambiguous -- e.g. missing reference modes, unclear cardinality, denormalized columns that could represent multiple facts. Output should be a draft OrmModel that a modeler can refine, with completeness warnings highlighting what could not be inferred."}

new_value:
{"description":"Add Import and Export workflow commands to the VS Code command palette.

**ORM: Import...** (quick pick):
- From Transcript -- existing LLM transcript extraction, renamed from 'Import Transcript'
- From dbt Project -- new: prompts for schema.yml files, runs importDbtProject + annotator, opens .orm.yaml

**ORM: Export...** (quick pick):
- As dbt Project -- renderDbt + annotateDbtExport, writes schema.yml + model SQL
- As DDL -- renderDdl, writes CREATE TABLE statements
- As Avro Schemas -- renderAvro, writes .avsc files

Also rename the existing 'ORM: Import Transcript' command to route through the Import picker.

Related: FREGMA-8fj (fix double ORM: prefix -- should be resolved as part of this work)."}
old_value: {"id":"FREGMA-4zm","title":"Add dbt import and export commands to command palette","description":"The VS Code command palette has no commands for dbt project import or any export operations (dbt schema, DDL, Avro). These capabilities exist in @fregma/core but are not wired into the extension's command palette. Should add commands like 'Import dbt Project', 'Export as dbt', 'Export as DDL', 'Export as Avro'.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"gschenz@ritchiebros.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T16:28:44Z","created_by":"Gabe Schenz","updated_at":"2026-02-27T16:28:44Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"Add Import and Export workflow commands to the VS Code command palette.

**ORM: Import...** (quick pick):
- From Transcript -- existing LLM transcript extraction, renamed from 'Import Transcript'
- From dbt Project -- prompts for DBT_PROJECT_DIR (folder picker), scans for schema.yml and sources.yml, runs importDbtProject + annotates originals in place, opens resulting .orm.yaml

**ORM: Export...** (quick pick):
- As dbt Project -- prompts for target DBT_PROJECT_DIR (folder picker), writes schema.yml + model SQL into the project's model directory, runs export annotator on generated files. Needs to understand dbt project structure (model paths, source naming).
- As DDL -- renderDdl, writes CREATE TABLE statements
- As Avro Schemas -- renderAvro, writes .avsc files

Design notes:
- Import scans the dbt project root for YAML files rather than asking for individual files
- Export writes INTO an existing dbt project rather than to a standalone output directory
- Both workflows should respect dbt_project.yml conventions where possible

Also rename the existing 'ORM: Import Transcript' command to route through the Import picker.

Related: FREGMA-8fj (fix double ORM: prefix -- should be resolved as part of this work)."}

new_value: Resolved in v0.2.1 prompt (PR #51). Identifier fact types now mandated; all three transcripts produce them correctly.
Resolved in v0.2.1 prompt (PR #51). No fabricated reference modes in v0.2.1 or v0.2.2 outputs.
Resolved in v0.2.1 prompt (PR #51). Both Mini and Codex include data type lengths on all text fields.
Resolved in v0.2.2 prompt (PR #53). Mini now produces 5-ary scheduling fact type with multiple uniqueness constraints.
Fixed in v0.2.2 prompt. Mini now produces composite uniqueness on Order+Product roles in ternary fact type.
Fixed in v0.2.2 prompt. No duplicate is_preferred constraints in any v0.2.2 outputs.
Fixed in v0.2.2 prompt. All identifier fact types now have both entity-role and value-role uniqueness constraints.
Fixed in v0.2.2 prompt. Customer places Order now has uniqueness on Order role (each Order placed by one Customer).
Resolved by v0.2.2 prompt improvements. LLM now produces constraint role references that the annotator matches without positional fallback.
Implemented YamlSourceMap and updated DiagnosticsProvider to resolve validation diagnostics to exact YAML source positions. PR #54.
Implemented role-level value constraint extraction in DraftModelParser. LLM can now emit values array in inferred_constraints, parser resolves role and applies constraint. PR #55.
Merged in PR #56. Batch import command implemented.

old_value:
{"id":"FREGMA-8ud","title":"Add @orm Copilot Chat participant for guided domain elicitation","description":"Implement a VS Code Chat Participant (@orm) that guides users through structured ORM domain elicitation directly in the Copilot Chat panel.

The participant would:
- Walk users through a structured interview (identify entities, reference modes, relationships, cardinality, constraints, value restrictions)
- Build the ORM model incrementally with feedback at each step
- Use the existing LLM extraction pipeline from @fregma/llm
- Generate the .orm.yaml file when the conversation is complete
- Solve both the 'what questions to ask' and 'import the result' problems in a single interaction

This is more impactful than a static conversation guide because it lives where engineers already work (Copilot Chat panel), provides real-time guidance, and doesn't require a separate transcript import step.

Requires the VS Code Chat Participant API (vscode.chat.createChatParticipant).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"gschenz@ritchiebros.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T01:58:40Z","created_by":"Gabe Schenz","updated_at":"2026-02-28T01:58:40Z"}
